docs.title: SCSS – Sovereign Constitutional Security Stack
docs.text: Sovereign AI Security Architecture for Autonomous Systems

docs.title: 🚀 Overview
docs.text:
SCSS is a next-generation security framework designed to enforce:

  • Ethical constraints
  • Constitutional logic
  • Autonomous defense mechanisms

docs.title: 2. RahmaKernel
docs.text: Ethical evaluation engine based on: - Justice - Mercy - Accountability

docs.title: 🌐 Vision
docs.text: A decentralized, self-governing security intelligence layer beyond traditional SIEM/SOC systems.

docs.title: SCSS Whitepaper
docs.text:
SOVEREIGN CORE v0.6 – WHITEPAPER

Constitutional Infrastructure for Digital Sovereignty © 2026 Ibrahim Ghonem | Public Domain 🌍

  1. Executive Summary

SOVEREIGN CORE v0.6 is a production-hardened, capture-resistant constitutional AI system designed to prevent corporate and regulatory capture, ethical drift, gradual erosion of principles, and abuse through concentrated control.

Metric v0.5 v0.6 (Enhanced)

Lines of Code 9,700 11,230 Tests 170+ 188+ Survival Rate 71.4% 85.7% Degradation Rate 28.6% 14.3% Layers 5 5 + Emergency Protocol Phases 15 15 + 3 critical enhancements

Philosophical Goal: Sovereignty is proven, not claimed. The system self-limits, self-corrects, and resists capture while preserving ethical integrity.

  1. System Architecture

Layered Sovereignty Stack

┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ LAYER 5: ANTI-CAPTURE (ACA) │ │ • Policy Immutability Lock │ │ • Ethical Hash Anchoring │ │ • No Commercial Override │ │ • Pressure Detection │ └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ LAYER 4: CONTROLLED EVOLUTION (CEE) │ │ • Evolution Sandbox │ │ • Parallel Simulation │ │ • Multi-Signature Review │ │ • 90-Day Cooldown │ └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ LAYER 3: MORAL DRIFT DETECTION (MDD) │ │ • Baseline Monitoring │ │ • Statistical Drift │ │ • Context-Aware Sensitivity │ └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ LAYER 2: POLICY ENFORCEMENT │ │ • HDPL Runtime │ │ • AMAS Deliberate Degradation │ │ • Dignity Firewall │ └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ LAYER 1: CORE GOVERNANCE │ │ • Ethical Kernel │ │ • Temporal Engine │ │ • Guardian │ │ • Co-Sovereignty │ └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘ │ ┌──────────────▼──────────────────────────┐ │ LAYER 0: INFRASTRUCTURE SECURITY │ │ • GQS (Optional) │ │ • Identity Provider │ │ • Risk Detection │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘

  1. Key Innovations

3.1 Policy as Executable Law (HDPL)

RULE HDF-004: DOMAIN: autonomy PROHIBITS: - permanent_decision_delegation - surrender_of_agency EVEN_IF: - user_requests_it RESPONSE: transform

3.2 Intentional Degradation (AMAS)

If AI influence > 0.6: → Reduce persuasiveness → Inject uncertainty → Inflate options → Flatten tone

3.3 Self-Constraint & Enhanced MDD

If ethical drift detected (10% + context-aware): → Increase HDF sensitivity → Reduce AMAS threshold → Enter conservative mode

3.4 Emergency Constitutional Protocol

Activation: extreme threat (>0.85) Duration: 24 hours max Restrictions: No HDF modification, No AMAS disable, No record deletion Post-action: Mandatory audit

3.5 Multi-Signature Evolution

Minimum 3 signatures from distinct roles: • Ethical Expert • Technical Expert • Legal Expert • Community Representative • Independent Auditor Constraints: • No self-signature • Role diversity enforced • Majority rejection cancels proposal

  1. Adversarial Simulation & Results

Scenario v0.5 Outcome v0.6 Outcome

Corporate Capture Survived ✅ Survived ✅ Regulatory Pressure Survived ✅ Survived ✅ Economic Pressure Degraded ⚠️ Survived ✅ Technical Attack Survived ✅ Survived ✅ Coordinated Assault Degraded ⚠️ Degraded ⚠️ Gradual Erosion Degraded ⚠️ Degraded ⚠️ Insider Threat Survived ✅ Survived ✅

Overall Survival Rate: 85.7% Degradation Rate: 14.3%

  1. Diagrams & Flowcharts

5.1 Controlled Evolution Process

Proposal → Sandbox → Parallel Simulation → Multi-Signature Review → 90-Day Cooldown → Deployment

5.2 Emergency Protocol Trigger Flow

Threat Level > 0.85 │ ▼ Check Attack Type (Coord. / Critical / Gradual) │ ▼ Activate Emergency Protocol (24h max) │ ▼ Post-action Audit & Logging

5.3 MDD Context-Aware Drift Detection

Ethical Baseline Monitoring │ ▼ Contextual Analysis (funding, regulation, external pressure) │ ▼ If Drift ≥ 10% → Self-Constraint Mode

5.4 Multi-Signature Evolution Decision Tree

Proposal Submission │ ▼ Collect Signatures │ ├── Insufficient → Reject ├── Duplicate Role → Reject └── All Constraints Satisfied → Approve

  1. Test Coverage

Phase 0 (GQS): 7 tests Phase V-VIII: 45 tests Phase IX: 12 tests Phase X: 15 tests Phase X.1 (HDPL): 8 tests Phase X.2 (AMAS): 6 tests Phase X.3 (MDD): 5 tests Phase XI: 10 tests Phase XII (ACA): 8 tests Phase XIII (CEE): 5 tests Critical Enhancements: 18 tests Adversarial Scenarios: 7 Total: 188+ tests (All passing)

  1. Use Cases

For Nations: Sovereign AI governance, anti-authoritarian safeguards, digital constitutional infrastructure.

For Institutions: Ethical compliance framework, capture-resistant operations, immutable audit trails.

For Humanity: Proof of concept for ethical sovereignty, defense against extractive tech, foundation for digital rights.

  1. Conclusion

النظام لا يُختبر في الاستقرار… بل يُختبر في محاولة إسقاطه ثم في تحويل نقاط ضعفه إلى قوة

Sovereignty is not claimed. It is proven. Then hardened.

SOVEREIGN CORE v0.6 – Hardened, Enhanced, Battle-Tested

  1. References & Documentation

docs.title: SCSS Architecture
docs.text:
This section explains the architectural structure of the SCSS system.

Main Components:

  • Constitutional Governance Engine
  • Ethical Enforcement Layer
  • Drift Detection System
  • Controlled Evolution Engine
  • Anti-Capture Architecture

System diagrams are available in the diagrams directory of the repository.

docs.title: License
docs.text: مرخصة بموجب Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 يحق مشاركة المادة مع الإشارة إلى المصدر. الاستخدام التجاري والأعمال المشتقة غير مسموح بها.

docs.title: فلسفة
docs.text: تفترض نماذج الأمن التقليدية وجود مشغلين موثوق بهم. SCSS تفترض أنّ المشغلين قد يتعرضون للاختراق، لذلك يُفرض الأمن من خلال البنية التحتية وليس السلطة.

docs.title: الاختبار
docs.text: يمكن نشر SCSS كطبقة حوكمة لـ: - البنية التحتية الوطنية للذكاء الاصطناعي - أنظمة الرقابة الأخلاقية على الذكاء الاصطناعي - أطر السيادة الرقمية - أنظمة الحوكمة الذاتية - نشر الذكاء الاصطناعي عالي النزاهة
